title: La Mayiza Consolidates ~90% of Former Chapitos Territory
detail: Reports indicate La Mayiza (Los Mayos faction) has consolidated control over approximately 90% of territory once dominated by Los Chapitos. 'Mayito Flaco' (Ismael Zambada García's son) is positioned to lead a reconstituted Sinaloa Cartel under a new leadership structure. Intelligence analysts note this shift may reduce fentanyl production as Mayos faction historically focused on cocaine and heroin rather than synthetic opioids.

title: US Intelligence: Fentanyl Operations Fragmenting After Cartel War
detail: US intelligence notes significant fragmentation of fentanyl operations as a result of the Sinaloa Cartel civil war. With Los Chapitos — the primary fentanyl producers — losing territorial control, analysts predict a potential shift in the US drug supply from fentanyl toward traditional drug markets including cocaine and heroin if La Mayiza fully consolidates power.
